intro: Pavetta loandensis, also known as the Loango Pavetta, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pavetta loandensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Pavetta loandensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can be found in various herbal formulations, including teas, tinctures, and syrups.
